message:
refactor(vc_collect): adopt asupersync::Outcome for structured cancellation

Migrate the entire collector subsystem from `Result<CollectResult,
CollectError>` to the three-variant `asupersync::Outcome` type, which
distinguishes Ok / Err / Cancelled at the type level. This enables
the runtime to tell apart "collection failed" from "collection was
cancelled by the user or a budget limit" without conflating the two
into a single error path.

Key changes:

- Introduce `CollectOutcome` type alias and `collect_checkpoint!` /
  `collect_try!` helper macros in vc_collect::lib for ergonomic
  cancellation-aware control flow.
- Add `checkpoint_reason()` function that checks `Cx::checkpoint()`
  and extracts a `CancelReason` when the context is cancelled.
- Update the `Collector` trait's `collect()` signature from
  `Result<CollectResult, CollectError>` to `CollectOutcome`.
- Instrument every collector (afsc, beads, caam, cass, caut,
  cloud_bench, dcg, github, mcp_mail, mod/dummy collectors, ntm, pt,
  rano, rch, sysmoni) with `collect_checkpoint!` calls at lifecycle
  boundaries: collect_start, pre/post command execution, pre/post
  parse, and collect_complete.
- Extend `SshRunner` with `exec_with_cx()` and
  `exec_timeout_with_cx()` methods that observe cancellation at
  connect, pre-command, and in-flight SSH channel wait boundaries,
  returning `SshError::Cancelled` when appropriate.
- Update `RemoteCollector::collect_remote()` to accept `&Cx` and
  propagate cancellation through SSH and parse phases.
- Add `machines_cancelled` counter to `CollectionSummary` and handle
  `Outcome::Cancelled` / `Outcome::Panicked` in result aggregation.
- Remove `ftui-harness` workspace dependency; inline the snapshot
  assertion helpers (`buffer_to_text`, `assert_buffer_snapshot`,
  `normalize`, `diff_text`, `snapshot_path_with_profile`) directly
  into `crates/vc_tui/tests/snapshot_screens.rs` and
  `tests/common/mod.rs`, eliminating the external build-time
  dependency on frankentui's harness crate.
- Update all affected tests to match on `Outcome::Ok` /
  `Outcome::Err` / `Outcome::Cancelled` variants.
- Add new unit tests: `checkpoint_reason` active/cancelled,
  `dummy_collector_returns_cancelled`, `exec_with_cx_cancelled`,
  `machine_collect_result_cancelled`, and
  `aggregate_results_cancelled_is_not_error`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
